items.headline: Draymond Green makes big prediction about Steve Kerr's future with Warriors
items.blurb: Draymond Green makes big prediction about Steve Kerr's future with Warriors — Green believes Kerr has coached his last game for the Golden State Warriors.

items.headline: Unlikely hero Luke Kennard leads Lakers to Game 1 win over Rockets
items.blurb: Unlikely hero Luke Kennard leads Lakers to Game 1 win over Rockets — With Luka Doncic and Austin Reaves both out of action and unlikely to return in the series, the Los Angeles Lakers were led by an unlikely hero on Saturday night.

items.headline: Five moves Warriors need to make to return to glory
items.blurb: Five moves Warriors need to make to return to glory — With a roster full of players on the wrong side of 30, two players recovering from serious knee injuries and multiple expiring contracts, the Warriors are at another transition point.

items.headline: All-Star duo leads Nuggets to ugly Game 1 win over Timberwolves
items.blurb: All-Star duo leads Nuggets to ugly Game 1 win over Timberwolves — The Denver Nuggets couldn't make threes, lost Aaron Gordon to foul trouble and fell behind by double digits early. Thanks to patience, defense and their All-Star duo, Denver took Game One over the Minnesota Timberwolves anyway.

items.headline: Hawks' CJ McCollum becomes newest MSG villain in Game 2 win over Knicks
items.blurb: Hawks' CJ McCollum becomes newest MSG villain in Game 2 win over Knicks — CJ McCollum took over for Trae Young as the Atlanta Hawks' starting point guard this season. In Monday's Game 2, he took over for Young as Madison Square Garden's No. 1 villain.

items.headline: Brandon Ingram disappears in Raptors' Game 2 loss to Cavaliers
items.blurb: Brandon Ingram disappears in Raptors' Game 2 loss to Cavaliers — When they traded for Brandon Ingram and gave him a big contract extension a year ago, the Toronto Raptors thought he was the missing piece. Monday night against the Cleveland Cavaliers, he was just missing.

items.headline: Hawks steal Game 2 off Alexander-Walker’s steal, Johnson’s slam
items.blurb: Hawks steal Game 2 off Alexander-Walker’s steal, Johnson’s slam — Watch as CJ McCollum knocks down the go-ahead bucket, then Nickeil Alexander-Walker forces a steal off Jalen Brunson and finds Jalen Johnson for the two-handed slam to give Atlanta a four-point lead with 10.2 seconds remaining.

items.headline: Nuggets’ Murray drains half-court heave to beat halftime buzzer
items.blurb: Nuggets’ Murray drains half-court heave to beat halftime buzzer — Watch as Denver Nuggets guard Jamal Murray drains a heave from half court to beat the halftime buzzer in Game 2 against the Minnesota Timberwolves.

items.headline: Why Raptors should feel encouraged despite turnover-ridden Game 2 loss
items.blurb: Why Raptors should feel encouraged despite turnover-ridden Game 2 loss — Michael Grange joins Danielle Michaud to discuss the Raptors’ Game 2 loss and why the 22 turnovers were the nail in the coffin despite an otherwise encouraging response to Game 1, and Brandon Ingram’s rough night on offence.

items.headline: McCollum leads late rally as Hawks stun Knicks to tie series
items.blurb: McCollum leads late rally as Hawks stun Knicks to tie series — CJ McCollum scored 32 points and the Atlanta Hawks rallied to stun the New York Knicks 107-106 on Monday night, tying their first-round playoff series at one game apiece.

items.headline: Ingram explains his poor performance from the field: ‘Shots weren’t falling’
items.blurb: Ingram explains his poor performance from the field: ‘Shots weren’t falling’ — Toronto Raptors forward Brandon Ingram discusses his rough shooting night in Game 2 against the Cleveland Cavaliers, explaining how the Cleveland defence affected him and that his shots simply weren’t “falling.”

items.headline: Rajakovic backs Ingram after rough Game 2: ‘He’s going to make his shots’
items.blurb: Rajakovic backs Ingram after rough Game 2: ‘He’s going to make his shots’ — Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic shows his support for Brandon Ingram after a rough shooting night in Game 2 against the Cleveland Cavaliers, saying Ingram is a very important player and that “he’s going to make his shots.”

items.headline: New-look Cavaliers backcourt is a resounding success in playoffs
items.blurb: New-look Cavaliers backcourt is a resounding success in playoffs — The Cleveland Cavaliers made dramatic changes to their starting backcourt at the trade deadline.

items.headline: Warriors Discussed Ivica Zubac Trade With Clippers
items.blurb: Warriors Discussed Ivica Zubac Trade With Clippers — The Golden State Warriors were one of the teams that discussed an Ivica Zubac trade with the Los Angeles Clippers before he was eventually traded to the Indiana Pacers. items.blurb: NBA Playoffs Live Tracker: Timberwolves vs. Nuggets, Game 2 — Fresh off an impressive Game 1 showing, Jamal Murray, Nikola Jokic and the Denver Nuggets look to gun for a 2-0 series lead in the first round against the Minnesota Timberwolves. Follow the action with Sportsnet’s live tracker at 10:30 p.m. ET / 7:30 p.m. PT.

items.headline: Raptors’ Rajakovic gets heated after refs award Cavs questionable timeout
items.blurb: Raptors’ Rajakovic gets heated after refs award Cavs questionable timeout — Watch as Toronto Raptors head coach Darko Rajakovic gets heated after the referees award the Cleveland Cavaliers a timeout when it was unclear if they actually had possession.

items.headline: Signs point to Steve Kerr leaving Golden State Warriors
items.blurb: Signs point to Steve Kerr leaving Golden State Warriors — Golden State Warriors coach Steve Kerr says he won't decide on his coaching future for "a week or two." Since the Warriors' season ended in a play-in loss to the Phoenix Suns on Friday, all the signs point to Kerr leaving the team he's coached for the last dozen seasons.

items.blurb: Al Horford Optimistic He'll Decide To Continue Playing Next Season — Golden State Warriors center Al Horford said Monday he will spend the next several weeks deciding whether to exercise his player option worth nearly $6 million for next season while leaving open the possibility of retirement. items.blurb: Brandin Podziemski Wants To Remain With Warriors Long-Term Ahead Of Extension Talks — Golden State Warriors guard Brandin Podziemski expressed a strong desire Monday to remain with the franchise that drafted him, saying he hopes to finalize a contract extension before next season's deadline in October. items.blurb: Warriors may look to elite CBB HC if they must replace Steve Kerr — Steve Kerr has been the head coach of the Golden State Warriors for 12 seasons, and it has been an incredible run.

items.blurb: Victor Wembanyama Unanimously Named NBA Defensive Player Of The Year — Victor Wembanyama has unanimously been named the 2025-26 NBA Defensive Player of the Year, becoming the youngest player in league history to win the award at the age of 22. items.headline: Joel Embiid Starts Recovery Program After Appendectomy, Timeline Uncertain
items.blurb: Joel Embiid Starts Recovery Program After Appendectomy, Timeline Uncertain — Philadelphia 76ers center Joel Embiid has begun a strength and conditioning program following his emergency appendectomy nearly two weeks ago, the team announced Monday. However, he remains in Philadelphia and is effectively ruled out for Tuesday's Game 2 against the Boston Celtics. The 76ers dropped Game 1 on Sunday, 123

items.headline: Taylor Jenkins Meets With Bucks To Discuss Head Coaching Vacancy
items.blurb: Taylor Jenkins Meets With Bucks To Discuss Head Coaching Vacancy — Taylor Jenkins met in Memphis last week with Jon Horst, Wes Edens and Jimmy Haslam to discuss the franchise's head coaching vacancy. The two sides are expected to meet again soon to further discuss the opening. Jenkins was head coach of the Memphis Grizzlies from 2019 until he was abruptly fired near the end of the 2024-25 season.

items.blurb: Jazz, Hawks Amongst Six NBA Draft Tiebreaker Winners — Six ties among teams with identical regular-season records were broken today through random drawings to determine the order of selection for NBA Draft 2026. items.headline: Draymond Green Says It 'Feels Like' Steve Kerr Has Coached His Last Game With Warriors
items.blurb: Draymond Green Says It 'Feels Like' Steve Kerr Has Coached His Last Game With Warriors — Draymond Green publicly predicted Monday that Steve Kerr's 12-year run as Golden State Warriors head coach has come to an end, three days after Kerr himself acknowledged uncertainty about his future with the franchise. "I hope he's our coach next season," Green said on his podcast, "The Draymond Green Show." "

items.headline: New Trail Blazers owner showing troubling signs of cheapness
items.blurb: New Trail Blazers owner showing troubling signs of cheapness — New Portland Trail Blazers owner Tom Dundon has only been officially in charge of his new team for three weeks, but he's already sending troubling signals that he's unwilling to spend money.

items.headline: Nets sign head coach Jordi Fernandez to multi-year extension
items.blurb: Nets sign head coach Jordi Fernandez to multi-year extension — Jordi Fernandez is sticking in Brooklyn for the long haul. The second-year head coach has reportedly signed a multi-year extension with the Nets, per ESPN’s Shams Charania on Monday.

items.blurb: Bulls Identify Six Candidates In President Of Basketball Operations Search — The Chicago Bulls have launched their search for a new head of basketball operations, receiving permission to interview five front office executives from around the league, sources told ESPN. items.blurb: Jordi Fernandez Signs Multiyear Extension With Nets — Jordi Fernandez and the entire coaching staff of the Brooklyn Nets have signed multiyear contract extensions with the franchise. Fernandez was hired by the Nets during the 2024 offseason as they pivoted to two full rebuilding seasons. items.blurb: Steve Ballmer Rejected Warriors' Pursuit Of Kawhi Leonard At Trade Deadline — The Golden State Warriors made a serious push to acquire Los Angeles Clippers forward Kawhi Leonard before the February trade deadline, only to receive a firm rejection from Clippers governor Steve Ballmer, league sources told ESPN. items.blurb: Mike Dunleavy Jr. Recently Signed Extension With Warriors — Mike Dunleavy Jr. signed an extension with the Golden State Warriors in recent months and has multiple seasons remaining on his contract. items.blurb: Steve Kerr 50-50 On Returning Next Season, Warriors Seek Multi-Year Commitment — The Golden State Warriors will want Steve Kerr to sign a multi-year extension if he decides he wants to continue as head coach next season. items.headline: CJ McCollum: Drops 32 points in win
items.blurb: CJ McCollum: Drops 32 points in win — McCollum tallied 32 points (12-22 FG, 3-10 3Pt, 5-7 FT), six assists, three rebounds, two steals and one block across 35 minutes in Monday's 107-106 Game 2 win over the Knicks in the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.

items.headline: Scottie Barnes: Leads Raps with 26 points in Game 2
items.blurb: Scottie Barnes: Leads Raps with 26 points in Game 2 — Barnes posted 26 points (11-19 FG, 1-4 3Pt, 3-4 FT), five assists, four rebounds, two steals and one block over 40 minutes during the Raptors' 115-105 Game 2 loss to the Cavaliers in the first round of the Eastern Conference playoffs. Visit RotoWire.com for more analysis on this update.
